name: GPT-4.1
note: API-only coding-focused flagship with a 1,047,576-token context window and June 2024 knowledge cutoff.

results.notes: [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]
GPQA Diamond [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

SWE-bench Verified [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement] [Corrected during full-corpus audit: OpenAI's own GPT-4.1 launch benchmark table reports 54.6% on SWE-bench Verified, not the rounded 55% currently recorded. Confirmed the precise 54.6% figure independently across multiple secondary write-ups that quote OpenAI's official table verbatim (futureagi.com, infoq.com, docsbot.ai's GPT-4.1-vs-mini/nano comparison pages, helicone.ai's developer guide) — all state 54.6%, never 54.6 rounded to]
AIME 2024; relayed from OpenAI's official GPT-4.1 launch benchmark table.
Also corroborated at 74.8% by docsbot.ai's GPT-4.1 comparison pages.
MathVista image reasoning; relayed from OpenAI's official launch table.
Listed as "Global MMLU" pass@1 in OpenAI's launch table.
Video-MME long-context video understanding, up 6.7 points from GPT-4o per OpenAI's launch table.
Princeton HAL 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, GPT-4.1 (April 2025 snapshot). GAIA validation set.

GPT-4.1 (flagship) scores 52.4% on the official Aider polyglot coding leaderboard (118/225 correct), confirmed via direct fetch of aider.chat's own leaderboard page — an independent third-party benchmark run, not an OpenAI self-report. Not currently tracked in this corpus entry.

IFEval instruction-following score for GPT-4.1 flagship (vs. GPT-4o's 81.0%), relayed from OpenAI's official GPT-4.1 launch benchmark table; confirmed via direct fetch of futureagi.com (already used as a source for this same model's SWE-bench figure in the corpus) and independently corroborated by infoq.com and docsbot.ai's GPT-4.1 comparison pages citing the identical 87.4%. Not currently tracked in this corpus entry.

verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2025-04-14 and $2/$8 per 1M token pricing, 1M context window via OpenAI docs and independent coverage. All 3 benchmark rows (MMLU 90.2, GPQA 66.3, SWE-bench 55) confirmed verbatim on the cited rdworldonline.com article. No changes made.
